title: Can We Produce Rice Without Harming Our Planet?
description: A Frontiers for Young Minds article seed about rice, food systems, land, water, energy, and emissions.

pages.text: Rice feeds more than half of the people on Earth, so rice production is both a food-security issue and an environmental issue. This article invites students to study agriculture as a system.
Students can track tradeoffs among land, water, energy, fertilizer, and greenhouse gases. The guiding question is not whether rice matters, but how people can produce it more sustainably.
The full article is CC BY 4.0 and works well with life-cycle thinking, climate solutions, and design challenges about improving food systems.
